- `GET /v1/mandates/{id}` — GET /v1/mandates/{id} (params: id*)
- `POST /v1/subscriptions` — POST /v1/subscriptions (params: Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/subscriptions` — GET /v1/subscriptions (params: customerId, status, limit, cursor)
- `GET /v1/subscriptions/{id}` — GET /v1/subscriptions/{id} (params: id*)
- `PATCH /v1/subscriptions/{id}` — PATCH /v1/subscriptions/{id} (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/subscriptions/{id}/events` — GET /v1/subscriptions/{id}/events (params: id*)
- `POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/pause` — POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/pause (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/resume` — POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/resume (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/cancel` — POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/cancel (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/resubscribe` — POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/resubscribe (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/change` — POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/change (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/subscriptions/{id}/upcoming-invoice` — GET /v1/subscriptions/{id}/upcoming-invoice (params: id*)
- `POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/schedule` — POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/schedule (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/subscriptions/{id}/schedule` — GET /v1/subscriptions/{id}/schedule (params: id*)
- `DELETE /v1/subscriptions/{id}/schedule` — DELETE /v1/subscriptions/{id}/schedule (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/discount` — POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/discount (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `DELETE /v1/subscriptions/{id}/discount` — DELETE /v1/subscriptions/{id}/discount (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/subscriptions/{id}/dunning` — GET /v1/subscriptions/{id}/dunning (params: id*)
- `GET /v1/subscriptions/{id}/dunning/attempts` — GET /v1/subscriptions/{id}/dunning/attempts (params: id*)
- `POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/payment-method` — POST /v1/subscriptions/{id}/payment-method (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/invoices/{id}` — GET /v1/invoices/{id} (params: id*)

- `POST /v1/invoices/{id}/void` — POST /v1/invoices/{id}/void (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)

- `GET /v1/webhooks/{id}` — GET /v1/webhooks/{id} (params: id*)
- `PATCH /v1/webhooks/{id}` — PATCH /v1/webhooks/{id} (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `DELETE /v1/webhooks/{id}` — DELETE /v1/webhooks/{id} (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `POST /v1/webhooks/{id}/rotate-secret` — POST /v1/webhooks/{id}/rotate-secret (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/webhooks/{id}/deliveries` — GET /v1/webhooks/{id}/deliveries (params: id*, limit, cursor, status, eventType, endpoint)
- `GET /v1/webhooks/{id}/deliveries/{deliveryId}` — GET /v1/webhooks/{id}/deliveries/{deliveryId} (params: id*, deliveryId*)
- `POST /v1/webhooks/{id}/deliveries/{deliveryId}/replay` — POST /v1/webhooks/{id}/deliveries/{deliveryId}/replay (params: id*, deliveryId*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/events/catalog` — GET /v1/events/catalog
- `GET /v1/events` — GET /v1/events (params: limit, cursor, type)
- `GET /v1/events/{id}` — GET /v1/events/{id} (params: id*)
- `GET /v1/settlements/escrow` — GET /v1/settlements/escrow
- `POST /v1/settlements/payout` — POST /v1/settlements/payout (params: Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/banks` — GET /v1/banks
- `GET /v1/payout-accounts` — GET /v1/payout-accounts
- `POST /v1/payout-accounts` — POST /v1/payout-accounts (params: Idempotency-Key*)
- `POST /v1/payout-accounts/resolve` — POST /v1/payout-accounts/resolve (params: Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/settlements` — GET /v1/settlements (params: limit, cursor, status)
- `GET /v1/settlements/{id}` — GET /v1/settlements/{id} (params: id*)
- `POST /v1/settlements/{id}/refund` — POST /v1/settlements/{id}/refund (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/organization` — GET /v1/organization
- `PUT /v1/organization` — PUT /v1/organization (params: Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/metrics/billing` — GET /v1/metrics/billing (params: from, to)
- `POST /v1/examples` — POST /v1/examples (params: Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/examples` — GET /v1/examples (params: kind, limit, cursor)
- `GET /v1/examples/{id}` — GET /v1/examples/{id} (params: id*)
- `POST /v1/sandbox/payment-methods` — POST /v1/sandbox/payment-methods (params: Idempotency-Key*)
- `POST /v1/sandbox/subscriptions/{id}/advance-cycle` — POST /v1/sandbox/subscriptions/{id}/advance-cycle (params: id*, Idempotency-Key*)
- `POST /v1/sandbox/webhooks/simulate` — POST /v1/sandbox/webhooks/simulate (params: Idempotency-Key*)
- `GET /v1/openapi.json` — GET /v1/openapi.json

All money fields are integer kobo (e.g. `250000` = ₦2,500.00).

---

## Error codes

- `CLIENT_INVALID_REQUEST` — The request could not be understood. Check the method, path, headers, and that the JSON body is well-formed before retrying.
- `CLIENT_VALIDATION_FAILED` — One or more fields are invalid. Read the `fields` map in this response — each key is a field path and its messages tell you exactly what to fix.
- `CLIENT_FORBIDDEN` — Your key is valid but not allowed to perform this action. Confirm the resource belongs to your organization and that your key has the required scope.
- `CLIENT_ROUTE_NOT_FOUND` — No route matches this method and path. Check the HTTP verb and the URL (including the `/v1` version prefix) against the API reference.
- `CLIENT_RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND` — No resource exists at that id in this mode. Verify the id and confirm your key targets the same mode (sandbox vs live) the resource lives in.
- `CLIENT_CONFLICT` — This request conflicts with the current state of the resource. Re-fetch it to see its latest state, then retry with values consistent with that state.
- `INVALID_CURSOR` — The pagination `cursor` is malformed or expired. Drop the `cursor` to start from the first page, then page forward using only the `nextCursor` values we return.
- `API_KEY_MISSING` — Send your secret key as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Create one in the dashboard under API keys if you do not have it.
- `API_KEY_INVALID` — That key is not recognized. Copy it fresh from the dashboard (it may have been rotated or revoked) and send the whole `nbo_sandbox_`/`nbo_live_` string with no extra whitespace.
- `API_KEY_SCOPE_FORBIDDEN` — Your key lacks the scope this endpoint needs. Grant the required scope (e.g. `customers:write`) to the key in the dashboard, or use a key that already has it.
- `API_KEY_ENVIRONMENT_MISMATCH` — A `nbo_live_` key is only accepted on the production deployment. Use your `nbo_sandbox_` key to build against sandbox, and switch to the `nbo_live_` key once you go live in production.
- `API_KEY_HOST_MISMATCH` — Your key's mode does not match the host you called. Send `nbo_sandbox_` keys to the sandbox host and `nbo_live_` keys to the live host — the base URL and the key prefix must agree.
- `IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_MISSING` — Send a unique `Idempotency-Key` header on this request; reuse the same key to safely retry the same operation.
- `IDEMPOTENCY_KEY_REUSED` — You reused an `Idempotency-Key` with a different request body. Use a new key for a new operation, or resend the identical body to replay the original result.
- `IDEMPOTENCY_IN_PROGRESS` — A request with this `Idempotency-Key` is still being processed. Wait briefly and retry with the same key to get the final result.
- `RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` — You're sending requests too quickly. Back off and retry after the interval in the `Retry-After` header.
- `PLATFORM_MAINTENANCE` — The platform is briefly paused for maintenance and is rejecting writes. Reads still work; retry your write after a short wait.
- `WEBHOOK_SIGNATURE_INVALID` — The webhook signature did not verify. Confirm you are using the correct signing secret for this endpoint and verifying against the exact raw request body (no re-serialization).
- `CUSTOMER_NOT_FOUND` — No customer exists with that id in this mode. Check the id, and confirm you are using the key for the same mode (sandbox vs live) the customer was created in.
- `CUSTOMER_EMAIL_TAKEN` — A customer with this email already exists in your organization. Reuse the existing customer, or create this one with a different email.
- `PLAN_NOT_FOUND` — No plan exists with that id in this environment. Verify the plan id, and that your key matches the environment the plan was created in.
- `PLAN_NAME_TAKEN` — A plan with this name already exists in your organization. Pick a different name, or update the existing plan instead of creating a new one.
- `PLAN_ALREADY_ARCHIVED` — This plan is already archived, so it cannot be archived or modified again. Unarchive it first if you need to change it.
- `PLAN_HAS_ACTIVE_SUBSCRIBERS` — You cannot archive or delete a plan that still has active subscriptions. Migrate or cancel those subscriptions first, then retry.
- `PRICE_NOT_FOUND` — No price exists with that id in this environment. Check the price id, and that your key matches the environment it was created in.
- `PRICE_PLAN_MISMATCH` — That price does not belong to the plan you referenced. Use a price that belongs to this plan, or reference the plan the price actually belongs to.
- `PRICE_ALREADY_INACTIVE` — This price is already inactive. No action is needed; create a new active price if you need one to subscribe against.
- `PRICE_TIERED_NOT_SUPPORTED` — Tiered/graduated pricing is not supported here. Use a flat per-unit price for this operation.
- `PAYMENT_METHOD_NOT_FOUND` — No payment method exists with that id for this customer. Verify the id, and that it belongs to the customer and environment you are calling with.
- `PAYMENT_METHOD_NOT_ACTIVE` — This payment method is not active (it may be pending, expired, or detached). Have the customer re-authorize or add a new payment method before charging.
- `PAYMENT_METHOD_KIND_MISMATCH` — This operation needs a different kind of payment method than the one provided (e.g. a card where a bank mandate was given). Supply a payment method of the required kind.
- `MANDATE_NOT_ACTIVE` — The mandate is not active yet (or has been revoked), so it cannot be charged. Wait for the customer to authorize it, or start a new mandate.
- `MANDATE_MAX_AMOUNT_EXCEEDED` — The charge is larger than the mandate's authorized maximum. Lower the amount, or have the customer authorize a new mandate with a higher limit.
- `MANDATE_CONSENT_PENDING` — The customer has not finished authorizing this mandate. Send them back through the consent link and wait for the activation webhook before charging.
- `SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND` — No subscription exists with that id in this environment. Check the id, and that your key matches the environment it was created in.
- `SUBSCRIPTION_ILLEGAL_TRANSITION` — The subscription cannot move to that state from its current one (e.g. resuming a canceled subscription). Re-fetch it to see its current status and choose a valid action.
- `SUBSCRIPTION_VERSION_CONFLICT` — The subscription changed since you loaded it (optimistic-concurrency conflict). Re-fetch it, reapply your change on the latest version, and retry.
- `SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_TERMINAL` — This action requires the subscription to be in a terminal state (e.g. canceled). Cancel or end it first, then retry.
- `SUBSCRIPTION_PAYMENT_METHOD_REQUIRED` — This subscription needs a usable payment method before it can be billed. Attach an active card or authorized mandate to the customer, then retry.
- `INVOICE_NOT_FOUND` — No invoice exists with that id in this environment. Verify the id, and that your key matches the environment it was created in.
- `INVOICE_ALREADY_FINALIZED` — This invoice is already finalized and its line items are locked. Issue a credit note or a new invoice instead of editing this one.
- `INVOICE_ALREADY_PAID` — This invoice is already paid, so it cannot be paid or voided again. If you need to reverse it, issue a refund against the payment.
- `INVOICE_NOT_VOIDABLE` — Only open, unpaid invoices can be voided. Because this invoice is paid or already void, issue a refund or credit note instead.
- `SUBSCRIPTION_SCHEDULE_NOT_FOUND` — No subscription schedule exists with that id. Check the id, or list schedules on the subscription to find the correct one.
- `SUBSCRIPTION_SCHEDULE_CONFLICT` — This schedule overlaps or conflicts with an existing one on the subscription. Cancel or adjust the conflicting schedule, then retry.
- `SUBSCRIPTION_SCHEDULE_INVALID_EFFECTIVE_AT` — The schedule's `effectiveAt` is invalid — it must be in the future and align with the billing cycle. Choose a valid future timestamp and retry.
- `PRORATION_NOT_APPLICABLE` — Proration does not apply to this change (for example, no mid-cycle difference to prorate). Proceed without proration, or verify the change you intended.
- `PRORATION_INTERVAL_SWITCH_UNSUPPORTED` — Prorating across a change of billing interval (e.g. monthly to yearly) is not supported. Schedule the interval change at the next period boundary instead.
- `COUPON_NOT_FOUND` — No coupon matches that id or code in this environment. Check the code for typos and that your key matches the environment the coupon was created in.
- `COUPON_EXPIRED` — This coupon is past its redeem-by date and can no longer be applied. Use a currently valid coupon, or issue a new one.
- `COUPON_MAX_REDEMPTIONS_REACHED` — This coupon has hit its maximum number of redemptions. Raise its redemption limit, or create a new coupon for further use.
- `COUPON_INVALID_DEFINITION` — The coupon's definition is inconsistent (e.g. both a percent and a fixed amount, or a non-positive value). Fix the definition so exactly one valid discount is specified.
- `COUPON_ALREADY_APPLIED` — This coupon is already applied to the subscription or invoice. Remove the existing discount first if you intend to reapply or change it.
- `DISCOUNT_NOT_FOUND` — No discount exists with that id on this subscription or invoice. Verify the id, or list discounts to find the correct one.
- `CREDIT_GRANT_NOT_FOUND` — No credit grant exists with that id for this customer. Check the id, and that it belongs to the customer and environment you are calling with.
- `CREDIT_GRANT_ALREADY_VOIDED` — This credit grant is already voided and cannot be voided or spent again. Issue a new credit grant if the customer needs more credit.
- `CREDIT_INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE` — The customer's credit balance is too low to cover this amount. Grant more credit, or apply a smaller amount.
- `CREDIT_INVALID_AMOUNT` — The credit amount is invalid — it must be a positive integer in the smallest currency unit. Send a valid amount and retry.
- `DUNNING_NO_OPEN_INVOICE` — There is no open, past-due invoice to run dunning against. Confirm the subscription actually has an unpaid invoice before triggering recovery.
- `DUNNING_ATTEMPT_NOT_FOUND` — No dunning attempt exists with that id. Verify the id, or list the attempts on the invoice to find the correct one.
- `DUNNING_CARD_UPDATE_REQUIRED` — The card needs customer action (such as OTP/3DS) that cannot be completed headlessly. Send the customer the hosted payment/checkout link to update or re-authorize their card.
- `DUNNING_ALREADY_TERMINAL` — This dunning process has already ended (recovered or exhausted) and cannot be advanced. Start a new billing attempt if the invoice is still unpaid.
- `SETTLEMENT_NOT_FOUND` — No settlement exists with that id in this environment. Verify the id, and that your key matches the environment it belongs to.
- `PAYOUT_ACCOUNT_MISSING` — Add the bank account you want to be paid into, then withdraw. Your balance is safe until you do — nothing is lost by adding it now.
- `REFUND_ALREADY_REFUNDED` — This charge has already been fully refunded. Inspect the existing refund; there is nothing left to refund.
- `REFUND_AMOUNT_EXCEEDS_NET` — The refund is larger than the remaining refundable (net) amount on the charge. Refund at most the remaining balance shown on the charge.
- `ESCROW_LOCKED` — These funds are held in escrow and cannot be paid out or refunded yet. Wait for the escrow hold to release, then retry.
- `PAYOUT_EXCEEDS_AVAILABLE` — The payout is larger than the available (non-escrowed, settled) balance. Reduce the amount to at most the available balance, or wait for more funds to settle.
- `QUOTA_EXCEEDED` — You've reached a plan or account quota for this operation. Back off and retry later, or contact support to raise the quota.
- `EXAMPLE_NOT_FOUND` — No example resource exists with that id in this environment. Check the id, and that your key matches the environment it was created in.
- `SYSTEM_INTERNAL_ERROR` — Something failed on our side. Retry shortly; if it persists, contact support with the `requestId` from the response `meta`.
- `SYSTEM_UPSTREAM_ERROR` — An upstream dependency was unavailable or timed out. This is transient — retry after a short backoff; if it persists, contact support with the `requestId`.
